question_html:
Make meaningful sentences with the following words:

Affluent, Delicate, Cleft, Portrait, Nemesis
solution_html:
Affluent: Their family is very affluent in this locality.

Delicate: The artist painted a delicate flower with such precision that it looked almost real.

Cleft: The mountain had a deep cleft that separated the two cliffs.

Portrait: Mishu painted a portrait of her daughter.

Nemesis: Despite his best efforts, the chess champion could not defeat his nemesis in the final round.

Marks Distribution:

For making meaningful correct sentences with the given words; (0.5×5)=2.5 marks.

question_html:
Read the following extract and answer the following question:

Some say the world will end in fire,

Some say in ice.

From what I’ve tasted of desire

I hold with those who favor fire.

But if it had to perish twice,

I think I know enough of hate

To say that for destruction ice

Is also great

And would suffice.

– Robert Frost

Identify and explain any one literary device used in the above extract.
solution_html:
One major literary device used in this poem is symbolism. Here, “fire” stands for desire—such as greed, passion, and ambition—while “ice” stands for hatred, coldness, and cruelty. These elements are not just about nature but symbolize destructive human emotions. Frost uses them to show that both powerful desire and deep hatred can lead to destruction, either of the world or of human values.

Marks Distribution:

For writing, one major literary device used in this poem is symbolism; 0.5 mark.

For writing, here, “fire” stands for desire—such as greed, passion, and ambition—while “ice” stands for hatred, coldness, and cruelty. These elements are not just about nature but symbolize destructive human emotions; 01 mark.

For writing, Frost uses them to show that both powerful desire and deep hatred can lead to destruction, either of the world or of human values; 01 mark.

[N.B: 0.5 mark will be deducted for two grammar or spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more grammar or spelling mistakes.]

question_html:
How is the transition to renewable energy sources like solar and wind critical for sustainable development?

Write a paragraph in approximately 100 words maintaining proper structure with a clear topic sentence, logical development, and a concluding sentence.
solution_html:
‘The Importance of Transitioning to Renewable Energy’

The shift from fossil fuels to renewable energy sources like solar and wind is not just necessary—it is urgent for building a sustainable future. These clean and abundant energy sources produce little to no greenhouse gases, helping to combat climate change and reduce environmental pollution. Unlike finite fossil fuels, solar and wind power are inexhaustible, ensuring a continuous supply of energy for generations to come. Moreover, investing in renewable energy stimulates economic growth by creating green jobs and reducing reliance on imported fuels. Therefore, embracing renewable energy is essential to meet today’s needs while protecting the planet and improving the quality of life for future generations.

Marks Distribution:

For writing the Title, “The Importance of Transitioning to Renewable Energy”; 0.5 mark.

For writing; Introductory sentence; 01 mark.

For writing, these clean and abundant energy sources produce little to no greenhouse gases, helping to combat climate change and reduce environmental pollution ;1.5 marks.

For writing, unlike finite fossil fuels, solar and wind power are inexhaustible, ensuring a continuous supply of energy for generations to come; 01 mark.

For writing conclusion; 01 mark.

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the obtained marks.

* 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two grammar or spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more grammar or spelling mistakes.]

[N.B. The student will get the allotted marks if he/she writes a proper alternative title.]
